name: Bill Barber
abstract: John William Barber (May 21, 1920 – June 18, 2007) was an American jazz tubist. He is considered by many to be the first person to play tuba in modern jazz.

concerts.label: Miles Davis with Gil Evans and Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: We had Bill Barber the first time on tuba, because he had been with--we heard him with Gil's thing with Miles.

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: There’s tuba players coming along, like Billy Butterfield and Bill Barber, who was the great one.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Bill Barber was there pretty much all of the time? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
